_comment: Countries shown as pins on the world map (reizen.html). 'iso' is a plain ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code (NL, BE, US, ...) — the same standard everyone uses, so it's never ambiguous and never needs 'figuring out'. That code is looked up directly in assets/data/world-map.json (pin position, auto-computed from real geography) and assets/data/countries/<ISO>.json (that country's own high-detail outline, shown when you click its pin) — see assets/js/modules/geo-render.js. It must also match the country name used in captions.json's 3rd caption field (case-insensitive, matched against 'name' below — see assets/js/modules/reizen-land.js).
_pinComment: 'pin' is OPTIONAL — by default a country's pin sits at the geographic centroid of its mainland (computed automatically, see STAPPENPLAN-REIZEN.md's data-pipeline notes). Set 'pin' to override that with a specific {lon, lat} instead — e.g. a capital city, easy to look up on Wikipedia/Google Maps, no pixel-guessing involved. Longitude first, then latitude (same order as GeoJSON).
_cityPinsComment: 'cityPins' is OPTIONAL per country — a map of city name (must match the 'Plaats' used in captions.json, case-insensitive) to its real-world {lon, lat}. Look the coordinates up once (Wikipedia's infobox, or right-click 'What's here' on Google Maps) and they'll always render in exactly the right spot on that country's map, at any zoom — no trial and error. Leave a city out and reizen-land.js falls back to an automatic fan-out layout for it instead; nothing breaks.
